name: 14-carat Cake with Vanilla Cream Cheese Frost
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, and ground cinnamon.
2. In a large bowl, beat eggs and add sugar. Let stand until sugar dissolves, about 10 minutes.
3. Stir in oil, grated raw carrots, drained canned crushed pineapple, and chopped nuts.
4. Pour the batter into greased and floured 9-inch round cake pans or a single (13 x 9-inch) pan.
5. Bake at 350°F for 35 to 40 minutes for layer cakes, or 55 minutes for the sheet cake, until it springs back when lightly touched.
6. Cool in the pans for about 10 minutes, then transfer to wire racks and cool completely.
To make frosting:
1. In a large bowl, combine softened cream cheese, vanilla, and butter. Beat until well blended.
2. Gradually add sifted powdered sugar, beating vigorously.
3. If the frosting is too thick, thin with milk until it reaches a spreading consistency.
4. Frost between layers, the top, and sides of the layer cake.
5. Frost the top and sides of the sheet cake.
